title: Security Management Process
description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. Includes risk analysis, risk management, sanction policy, and information system activity review.
purpose: Establishes the foundational risk management framework for protecting ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Conduct a thorough, accurate, and scope-appropriate risk analysis of all potential risks and vulnerabilities to ePHI. Implement a risk management programme with security measures to reduce risks to a reasonable and appropriate level. Establish a sanction policy for workforce members who violate security policies. Implement regular review of information system activity logs.
evidenceExamples: Risk management plan with treatment measures
Sanction policy with documented enforcement actions
bestPractices: Conduct risk analysis annually and after significant changes
Use NIST SP 800-30 methodology for risk analysis
Document risk analysis methodology, data collection, and findings comprehensively

title: Assigned Security Responsibility
description: Identify the security official responsible for developing and implementing the policies and procedures required by HIPAA Security Rule.
purpose: Establishes clear accountability for HIPAA Security Rule compliance.
implementationGuidance: Designate a Security Officer with authority and responsibility for HIPAA security compliance. Document the designation formally. Ensure the Security Officer has sufficient resources, authority, and expertise.
evidenceExamples: Job description with HIPAA security responsibilities
Organisational chart showing Security Officer position
bestPractices: Designate a Security Officer with appropriate qualifications (CISSP, HCISPP)
Ensure Security Officer has direct access to senior leadership
Clearly distinguish Security Officer from Privacy Officer responsibilities

title: Workforce Security
description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure all workforce members have appropriate access to ePHI and prevent those who do not have access from obtaining ePHI. Includes authorisation, clearance, and termination procedures.
purpose: Ensures only authorised workforce members access ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Implement workforce authorisation procedures for granting ePHI access. Establish clearance procedures including background checks where appropriate. Define termination procedures to revoke all access upon separation.
bestPractices: Automate access revocation upon workforce termination
Conduct background checks for all roles with ePHI access
Review workforce access authorisations at least annually

title: Information Access Management
description: Implement policies and procedures for authorising access to ePHI consistent with the Privacy Rule minimum necessary standard and the requirements of the Security Rule.
purpose: Ensures ePHI access is granted based on need and job function.
implementationGuidance: Implement access authorisation policies. Establish access establishment and modification procedures based on role changes. Apply the minimum necessary standard to all ePHI access grants.
evidenceExamples: Access modification records following role changes
bestPractices: Implement RBAC aligned to job functions and minimum necessary standard
Review access rights quarterly for all users with ePHI access
Automate access modification upon role changes

title: Security Awareness and Training
description: Implement a security awareness and training programme for all workforce members including management. Includes protection from malicious software, log-in monitoring, and password management.
purpose: Ensures all workforce members are equipped to protect ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Develop and implement annual security awareness training for all workforce members. Provide training on recognising malicious software, protecting passwords, and monitoring login attempts. Conduct periodic reminders and additional training as environment changes.
evidenceExamples: Annual training completion records for all staff
bestPractices: Conduct annual training and periodic refreshers (at least quarterly reminders)
Test comprehension with post-training assessments
Tailor training to different workforce roles

title: Security Incident Procedures
description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents, including identifying, responding to, and mitigating known security incidents, and documenting security incidents and outcomes.
purpose: Ensures security incidents affecting ePHI are managed effectively.
implementationGuidance: Define security incident procedures covering identification, reporting, response, and documentation. Establish an incident response team. Define escalation procedures. Document all incidents and responses. Conduct post-incident reviews.
bestPractices: Maintain an incident log documenting all security incidents and outcomes
Test incident response procedures annually
Report significant incidents to HHS as required by HIPAA Breach Notification Rule

title: Contingency Plan
description: Establish policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems that contain ePHI. Includes data backup, disaster recovery, emergency mode operation, and testing.
purpose: Ensures ePHI can be recovered and business operations can continue following a disaster.
implementationGuidance: Develop a data backup plan with regular, tested backups of all ePHI. Define a disaster recovery plan for restoring systems. Establish emergency mode operation procedures. Conduct testing and revision of contingency plans. Develop applications and data criticality analysis.
bestPractices: Test data recovery at least semi-annually
Define RTO and RPO for all critical ePHI systems
Store backups offsite and test retrieval from offsite storage

title: Evaluation
description: Perform a periodic technical and non-technical evaluation of the security safeguards to determine the extent to which the Security Rule is met.
purpose: Ensures HIPAA security safeguards remain effective over time.
implementationGuidance: Conduct regular evaluations (internal or external) of all HIPAA Security Rule safeguards. Conduct evaluations when significant operational or environmental changes occur. Document evaluation findings and address identified gaps.
bestPractices: Conduct formal HIPAA Security Rule evaluations at least annually
Engage third-party evaluators for objective assessment
Address all evaluation findings within defined timeframes

title: Business Associate Contracts
description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI on the covered entity's behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the ePHI.
purpose: Extends HIPAA security requirements to business associates handling ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Identify all business associates. Execute a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with each. Ensure BAAs include all required provisions. Review BAAs annually. Verify business associate HIPAA compliance.
evidenceExamples: Executed BAAs with all business associates
bestPractices: Execute BAAs before sharing any ePHI
Include HIPAA-required provisions in all BAAs
Request compliance evidence from high-risk business associates

title: Facility Access Controls
description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facility in which they are housed to authorised users, while ensuring that properly authorised access is allowed.
purpose: Prevents unauthorised physical access to systems containing ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Implement contingency operations procedures for facility access during emergencies. Develop a facility security plan with physical access controls. Implement access control and validation procedures. Maintain maintenance records for physical access control systems.
requiredDocuments: Maintenance records for physical access systems
bestPractices: Implement electronic badge access with audit logging for all ePHI server areas
Review physical access rights quarterly
Monitor and log all after-hours physical access

title: Facility Access — Contingency Operations
description: Establish and implement procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.
purpose: Ensures critical personnel can access facilities during emergencies to restore ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Define contingency access procedures that allow authorised personnel to access facilities during emergencies. Document emergency access authorisation. Test procedures as part of contingency plan testing.
bestPractices: Test contingency access procedures during disaster recovery exercises
Maintain emergency access credentials in a secure, accessible location
Document all use of emergency access procedures

title: Workstation Use
description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation that accesses ePHI.
purpose: Ensures workstations are used appropriately to protect ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Define acceptable use of workstations that access ePHI. Specify physical environmental requirements (e.g., screen positioned to prevent visual observation). Restrict workstation to necessary functions only.
bestPractices: Position workstations to prevent unauthorised viewing of ePHI
Lock screens after defined inactivity periods
Enforce workstation use policies through technical controls

title: Workstation Security
description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI to restrict access to authorised users.
purpose: Physically protects workstations from unauthorised access.
implementationGuidance: Implement physical security controls for workstations (cable locks, screen filters, secure storage). Restrict workstation access to authorised personnel. Implement automatic workstation locking.
bestPractices: Secure all unattended workstations with cable locks
Require screen privacy filters for workstations in public areas
Configure automatic screen lock after 5 minutes of inactivity

title: Device and Media Controls
description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.
purpose: Controls the physical movement of media and devices containing ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Define procedures for receiving and disposing of hardware and media with ePHI. Track movement of devices and media containing ePHI. Implement data backup prior to moving equipment. Implement media sanitisation procedures for disposal.
requiredDocuments: Data backup procedures for media movement
bestPractices: Maintain a complete inventory of all media containing ePHI
Use certified destruction services for ePHI media disposal
Obtain destruction certificates for all disposed media

title: Access Controls
description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programmes that have been granted access rights. Includes unique user identification, emergency access, automatic logoff, and encryption.
purpose: Ensures technical controls restrict ePHI access to authorised users only.
implementationGuidance: Assign unique user identifiers to each user. Implement emergency access procedures for critical situations. Configure automatic logoff after defined inactivity. Implement encryption and decryption mechanisms for ePHI.
evidenceExamples: Encryption at rest and in transit implementation evidence
bestPractices: Implement MFA for all user access to ePHI
Configure logoff after 15 minutes of inactivity for workstations accessing ePHI
Use AES-256 encryption for all stored ePHI

title: Unique User Identification
description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.
purpose: Enables accountability by tracking which user performed each action involving ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Configure all systems to require unique user identifiers. Prohibit shared accounts for individuals. Enforce unique ID assignment in all systems accessing ePHI.
evidenceExamples: User account configuration showing unique IDs
Prohibition of shared accounts documented and enforced
Audit trail demonstrating unique user actions
bestPractices: Implement identity governance to manage unique IDs across all systems
Enforce unique ID requirement through technical controls
Conduct quarterly audits for shared or generic accounts

title: Emergency Access Procedure
description: Establish and implement as needed procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency.
purpose: Ensures critical ePHI is accessible during emergency situations.
implementationGuidance: Define break-glass emergency access procedures for critical ePHI. Limit emergency access to situations where normal access is unavailable. Log all emergency access for post-incident review.
bestPractices: Implement formal break-glass procedures with mandatory justification
Log and alert on all break-glass access
Review emergency access logs weekly

title: Automatic Logoff
description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.
purpose: Prevents unauthorised access to ePHI through unattended sessions.
implementationGuidance: Configure automatic logoff or screen lock after defined inactivity periods. Apply across all systems and devices accessing ePHI. Define appropriate inactivity timeouts based on risk (e.g., 15 minutes for workstations).
requiredDocuments: Implementation evidence across all ePHI systems
evidenceExamples: Auto-logoff configuration for all ePHI-accessing systems
bestPractices: Set logoff timeout to 15 minutes or less for workstations
Apply shorter timeouts to high-risk environments (e.g., clinical areas)
Test auto-logoff configuration compliance periodically

title: Encryption and Decryption
description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.
purpose: Protects ePHI from unauthorised disclosure through encryption.
implementationGuidance: Implement encryption for ePHI at rest using AES-256 or equivalent. Implement encryption in transit using TLS 1.2+. Manage encryption keys securely. Verify encryption implementation covers all ePHI storage and transmission channels.
evidenceExamples: TLS 1.2+ implementation evidence for ePHI transmission
bestPractices: Use AES-256 for all ePHI at rest
Require TLS 1.2 or higher for all ePHI in transit
Implement envelope encryption with a KMS for database ePHI

title: Audit Controls
description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.
purpose: Creates accountability for ePHI access and enables investigation of security incidents.
implementationGuidance: Implement comprehensive audit logging on all systems containing ePHI. Log user access, modifications, and deletions of ePHI. Conduct regular audit log reviews. Protect audit logs from modification.
requiredDocuments: Audit logging configuration for ePHI systems
evidenceExamples: Audit log configuration showing ePHI access logging
bestPractices: Review ePHI audit logs at least weekly for anomalous access
Implement automated alerts for suspicious ePHI access patterns
Retain ePHI audit logs for at least 6 years (HIPAA record retention requirement)

title: Integrity Controls
description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.
purpose: Ensures ePHI is not improperly modified or destroyed.
implementationGuidance: Implement technical mechanisms to verify ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorised manner. Use checksums, digital signatures, or audit logging to detect tampering. Monitor for unexpected modifications.
bestPractices: Implement file integrity monitoring for critical ePHI stores
Use digital signatures for high-sensitivity ePHI
Alert immediately on detected integrity violations

title: Person or Entity Authentication
description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.
purpose: Ensures only authenticated individuals access ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Implement strong authentication for all users accessing ePHI. Implement multi-factor authentication for high-risk access. Verify identity before granting access to sensitive ePHI.
bestPractices: Require MFA for all workforce access to ePHI
Use phishing-resistant MFA for privileged access
Verify identity rigorously during password reset procedures

title: Transmission Security
description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorised access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
purpose: Protects ePHI from interception and unauthorised access during transmission.
implementationGuidance: Implement encryption for all ePHI transmitted over open networks. Use TLS 1.2 or higher. Implement integrity controls for ePHI in transit. Verify all transmission paths for ePHI are encrypted.
requiredDocuments: Encryption requirements for ePHI in transit
Transmission path inventory with security status
evidenceExamples: TLS configuration evidence for all ePHI transmission paths
Network scanning showing no unencrypted ePHI transmission
bestPractices: Prohibit transmission of unencrypted ePHI over any network
Use TLS 1.2 or higher for all ePHI in transit
Implement end-to-end encryption for ePHI shared with external parties

title: Security Standards: General Rules
description: Covered entities and business associates must ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all ePHI they create, receive, maintain, or transmit; protect against reasonably anticipated threats; and ensure compliance by the workforce.
purpose: Establishes the three fundamental HIPAA Security Rule objectives: confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.
implementationGuidance: Implement safeguards addressing all three objectives: confidentiality (preventing unauthorised access), integrity (preventing improper modification), and availability (ensuring authorised users can access ePHI when needed). Conduct risk analysis to identify threats to each objective.
requiredDocuments: Risk analysis addressing CIA triad for ePHI
evidenceExamples: Risk analysis covering confidentiality, integrity, and availability risks
Implemented safeguards mapped to each CIA objective
bestPractices: Treat confidentiality, integrity, and availability as equally important objectives
Map all safeguards to specific CIA threats identified in the risk analysis
Measure and report on CIA safeguard effectiveness to leadership

title: Flexibility of Approach
description: In meeting the standards, covered entities and business associates may use any security measures that reasonably and appropriately implement the standards and implementation specifications.
purpose: Allows organisations to implement HIPAA controls in a way proportionate to their size, complexity, and risk.
implementationGuidance: Document the rationale for control selections based on your organisation's specific risk analysis, size, complexity, and capabilities. Demonstrate that selected controls address identified risks. Review control appropriateness periodically.
evidenceExamples: Documented rationale for each control implementation approach
Risk analysis showing how selected controls address identified risks
bestPractices: Document rationale for each control choice, especially where you deviate from common practices
Use risk analysis to justify control selections and priorities
Review control appropriateness after significant organisational changes

title: Policies and Procedures
description: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of the Security Rule.
purpose: Ensures HIPAA Security Rule compliance is implemented through documented, enforceable policies.
implementationGuidance: Develop comprehensive security policies and procedures addressing all HIPAA Security Rule requirements. Ensure policies are reviewed and updated at least annually or when significant changes occur.
requiredDocuments: HIPAA security policies and procedures library
bestPractices: Maintain a complete, organised HIPAA security policy library
Review all policies annually and update when the security environment changes
Obtain workforce acknowledgement for all security policies

title: Documentation
description: Maintain written (which may be electronic) policies and procedures. If an action, activity, or assessment is required, maintain a written record of the action, activity, or assessment.
purpose: Creates the documentary evidence required to demonstrate HIPAA compliance.
implementationGuidance: Document all HIPAA security policies, procedures, actions, activities, and assessments. Retain documentation for at least 6 years from creation or last effective date. Protect documentation from unauthorised access or modification.
bestPractices: Maintain HIPAA documentation for at least 6 years minimum
Organise documentation to enable rapid retrieval for OCR investigations
Implement version control for all policies and procedures

title: Breach Notification — General Rule
description: A covered entity shall notify each individual whose unsecured PHI has been, or is reasonably believed by the covered entity to have been, accessed, acquired, used, or disclosed as a result of a breach.
purpose: Ensures individuals are informed when their PHI has been breached.
implementationGuidance: Define breach notification procedures. Notify affected individuals within 60 days of discovering a breach. Provide required notice content. Notify HHS. For breaches of 500+ individuals in a state, notify prominent media outlets.
evidenceExamples: Media notification records for large breaches
bestPractices: Notify individuals as soon as possible after discovery, not just by Day 60
Pre-draft breach notification letter templates
Submit all breaches to HHS annually (small breaches) or within 60 days (large breaches)

title: Breach Notification — Definitions
description: A breach is defined as an acquisition, access, use, or disclosure of PHI in a manner not permitted under the Privacy Rule that compromises the security or privacy of the PHI, unless a risk assessment using specified factors demonstrates low probability the PHI was compromised.
purpose: Defines when breach notification is required.
implementationGuidance: Conduct a four-factor risk assessment for all potential breaches to determine if notification is required: (1) nature and extent of PHI involved; (2) who used or to whom PHI was disclosed; (3) whether PHI was actually acquired or viewed; (4) extent to which risk has been mitigated.
evidenceExamples: Four-factor risk assessment for potential breaches
bestPractices: Document every suspected breach with a formal four-factor risk assessment
Engage legal counsel for complex breach determinations
Err on the side of notification when the four-factor assessment is inconclusive

title: Notification to Media
description: For breaches affecting more than 500 residents of a state or jurisdiction, the covered entity must notify prominent media outlets serving the state or jurisdiction.
purpose: Ensures widespread public notification for large breaches affecting communities.
implementationGuidance: Define threshold monitoring for breach size. Establish procedures to identify prominent media outlets in each state/jurisdiction. Prepare media notification procedures. Ensure notification occurs without unreasonable delay (within 60 days).
evidenceExamples: Media notification records for large breaches
bestPractices: Prepare media relations procedures in advance for large breach scenarios
Coordinate media notification with legal counsel and PR
Notify media in coordination with (not before) individual notifications

title: Notification to the Secretary
description: A covered entity shall notify the Secretary of breaches of unsecured PHI. Large breaches (500+ individuals) must be reported within 60 days. Small breaches must be reported annually.
purpose: Enables HHS to monitor and enforce breach notification compliance.
implementationGuidance: Establish HHS breach reporting procedures. For large breaches (500+ individuals), report to HHS within 60 days of discovery. For small breaches (under 500 individuals), maintain a log and submit annually by March 1.
bestPractices: Submit large breach notifications to HHS simultaneously with individual notifications
Maintain a comprehensive breach log throughout the year for annual submission
Review the HHS breach portal regularly for process updates

title: De-identification of PHI
description: PHI can be de-identified using the Expert Determination or Safe Harbor method, after which it is no longer PHI subject to HIPAA.
purpose: Enables use of PHI for research, analytics, and operations without HIPAA restrictions after de-identification.
implementationGuidance: Apply either Expert Determination (a qualified expert determines re-identification risk is very small) or Safe Harbor (remove 18 specific identifiers) to de-identify PHI. Document the de-identification method and outcome.
requiredDocuments: Expert determination records OR Safe Harbor checklist
bestPractices: Use Safe Harbor method for standard de-identification requirements
Engage a qualified expert for Expert Determination
Test de-identified data for re-identification risk before wide use

title: Access of Individuals to PHI
description: Individuals have a right to inspect and obtain a copy of their PHI, with limited exceptions.
purpose: Enables patients to access their health records.
implementationGuidance: Implement procedures to receive and process individual access requests within 30 days (extendable to 60 days). Provide electronic copies when requested. Do not charge more than a reasonable, cost-based fee. Allow individuals to direct PHI to third parties.
evidenceExamples: Individual access request records within 30-day deadline
bestPractices: Aim to fulfil access requests within 15 days
Provide electronic access (patient portal) for convenient self-service
Audit access request response times monthly

title: Amendment of PHI
description: Individuals have the right to request amendment of their PHI if they believe the PHI is incorrect or incomplete.
purpose: Enables patients to correct inaccurate health records.
implementationGuidance: Implement procedures to receive and respond to amendment requests within 60 days. Document grounds for denial. Notify the individual of denial. Provide a process for the individual to submit a statement of disagreement.
bestPractices: Respond to amendment requests within 30 days where possible
Train staff on when amendments must be accepted vs. when they can be denied
Propagate accepted amendments to all relevant records

title: Accounting of Disclosures
description: Individuals have a right to request an accounting of disclosures of PHI made by the covered entity for the previous six years.
purpose: Provides patients with transparency about how their PHI has been disclosed.
implementationGuidance: Maintain a log of all PHI disclosures that require accounting. Respond to accounting requests within 60 days. Note that disclosures for TPO (Treatment, Payment, Operations) are excluded from the accounting.
evidenceExamples: Accounting of disclosures provided to individuals
bestPractices: Maintain a running disclosure log to simplify accounting request responses
Train staff on which disclosures require logging for accounting purposes
Review disclosure log completeness quarterly

title: Administrative Requirements — Privacy Rule
description: Covered entities must designate a Privacy Officer, train the workforce, have safeguards, have a complaint process, and not retaliate against individuals who exercise their rights.
purpose: Establishes the administrative infrastructure for HIPAA Privacy Rule compliance.
implementationGuidance: Designate a Privacy Officer. Implement workforce privacy training. Develop and document privacy safeguards. Establish a complaint process. Prohibit retaliation against individuals who exercise their rights.
bestPractices: Designate separate Privacy and Security Officers
Provide annual privacy training to all workforce
Investigate and respond to all privacy complaints promptly

title: Uses and Disclosures — Organisational Requirements
description: Covered entities must comply with privacy requirements when using or disclosing PHI, including requirements for hybrid entities, affiliated covered entities, and business associates.
purpose: Extends HIPAA privacy requirements to complex organisational structures.
implementationGuidance: Assess organisational structure for HIPAA compliance requirements. For hybrid entities, define the healthcare component. For affiliated entities, consider joint privacy notices and accountability. Ensure all BAAs are in place.
bestPractices: Conduct annual review of organisational structure for HIPAA compliance implications
Ensure all subsidiaries and affiliates handling PHI are covered by appropriate agreements
Seek legal counsel for complex corporate structure HIPAA questions

title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Healthcare Operations
description: A covered entity may use or disclose PHI for its own treatment, payment, and healthcare operations without patient authorisation.
purpose: Enables the core functions of healthcare delivery without burdensome consent requirements.
implementationGuidance: Define what constitutes TPO for your organisation. Ensure disclosures for TPO comply with the minimum necessary standard. Document TPO uses and disclosures in privacy policies.
requiredDocuments: Minimum necessary standard for TPO disclosures
bestPractices: Define specific TPO purposes to guide workforce on permissible disclosures
Apply minimum necessary standard even to TPO disclosures
Train workforce on the boundaries of TPO

title: Uses and Disclosures Requiring Opportunity to Agree or Object
description: Covered entities may use or disclose PHI in certain situations only after providing individuals with the opportunity to agree or object (e.g., facility directories, disclosures to family members).
purpose: Provides individuals with control over certain PHI disclosures in healthcare settings.
implementationGuidance: Implement procedures to provide patients with opportunity to agree or object before using PHI for facility directories or disclosing to family/friends. Document patient preferences. Accommodate incapacitated patients through professional judgment.
requiredDocuments: Facility directory and family disclosure procedures
evidenceExamples: Staff training on seeking patient agreement/objection
bestPractices: Ask for patient preferences upon registration
Document preferences in the medical record
Train all clinical staff on when to seek patient agreement or objection

title: Uses and Disclosures for Which an Authorisation Is Not Required
description: Covered entities may use or disclose PHI without patient authorisation for specific purposes including public health activities, law enforcement, research, and national security.
purpose: Enables important public health, research, and law enforcement activities.
implementationGuidance: Identify applicable disclosure exceptions and implement supporting procedures. Ensure disclosures under exceptions are limited to minimum necessary. Document all disclosures under each exception. Seek legal guidance for complex situations.
evidenceExamples: Procedure documentation for each applicable exception
Legal guidance records for complex disclosures
bestPractices: Document the specific exception basis for each disclosure
Apply minimum necessary to exception disclosures where required
Seek legal counsel before making novel exception-based disclosures

title: HITECH Act — Strengthened Enforcement
description: The HITECH Act increased penalties for HIPAA violations based on level of culpability. Penalties range from $100 per violation (did not know) to $50,000+ (willful neglect). Maximum is $1.5M per category per year.
purpose: Strengthens HIPAA enforcement through higher financial penalties.
implementationGuidance: Implement a robust HIPAA compliance programme to avoid violations. In particular, address willful neglect (failure to take reasonable steps) which carries the highest penalties. Demonstrate active compliance efforts to mitigate potential penalties.
evidenceExamples: Regular security assessments demonstrating due diligence
Corrective action records for identified gaps
bestPractices: Address all identified HIPAA gaps promptly to avoid willful neglect findings
Document compliance efforts comprehensively for use in penalty mitigation
Implement a mature HIPAA compliance programme with executive oversight

title: HITECH Act — Extension to Business Associates
description: HITECH made HIPAA Security Rule provisions directly applicable to business associates, not just through contractual obligations. Business associates are now directly liable for HIPAA Security Rule compliance.
purpose: Extends direct HIPAA Security Rule liability to business associates.
implementationGuidance: If you are a business associate, comply directly with the HIPAA Security Rule as a regulatory obligation. Implement the same safeguards as a covered entity. Execute BAAs with sub-contractors. Notify covered entities of breaches within 60 days.
requiredDocuments: BA HIPAA Security Rule compliance programme
Breach notification procedures to covered entities
evidenceExamples: Breach notification records to covered entities
bestPractices: Treat HIPAA as a direct obligation, not just a contractual one
Implement the same safeguards as a covered entity
Notify covered entities of breaches promptly to meet their notification deadlines

title: HITECH Act — Accounting of Disclosures from EHRs
description: HITECH expands the right to an accounting of disclosures to include disclosures for TPO when made through an electronic health record, which HIPAA had previously excluded.
purpose: Increases patient transparency over how electronic health records are accessed.
implementationGuidance: Implement access logs for EHR systems. Upon patient request, provide an accounting of disclosures from the EHR for up to 3 years prior. This extends the accounting right beyond the baseline HIPAA requirement.
bestPractices: Enable comprehensive audit logging in EHR systems
Maintain EHR access logs for at least 3 years
Test accounting request fulfilment periodically to ensure completeness

title: Minimum Necessary Standard
description: Covered entities must make reasonable efforts to limit PHI use and disclosure to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose.
purpose: Reduces unnecessary exposure of PHI by limiting access and disclosure to what is actually needed.
implementationGuidance: Implement minimum necessary standard for all uses and disclosures of PHI. Define what constitutes minimum necessary for routine disclosures. Evaluate non-routine requests individually. Apply to workforce access to PHI.
evidenceExamples: Minimum necessary definitions for common use cases
Access controls limiting PHI access to minimum necessary
bestPractices: Define minimum necessary for each common PHI use and disclosure scenario
Implement RBAC to technically enforce minimum necessary for system access
Train workforce to apply minimum necessary in every interaction involving PHI

title: Notice of Privacy Practices
description: Covered entities must provide a Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP) to individuals at the time of first service delivery or upon request.
purpose: Informs patients how their PHI will be used and their rights.
implementationGuidance: Develop and maintain an accurate NPP. Provide NPP at first service encounter. Post NPP prominently in facilities and on websites. Obtain written acknowledgement of receipt where required. Update NPP when practices change.
evidenceExamples: Current NPP meeting all required content
NPP posted in facility and on website
bestPractices: Review NPP annually for accuracy and regulatory compliance
Obtain signed acknowledgement at first patient encounter
Update NPP and notify patients when privacy practices change

title: Authorisation for Uses and Disclosures
description: For uses and disclosures not permitted by the Privacy Rule, covered entities must obtain valid written authorisation from the individual.
purpose: Gives patients control over PHI uses and disclosures not necessary for treatment.
implementationGuidance: Define when patient authorisation is required (research, marketing, sale of PHI). Implement compliant authorisation forms. Verify authorisation validity before processing. Honour revocations promptly.
bestPractices: Develop clear, understandable authorisation forms using plain language
Train staff on when authorisation is required
Process revocations immediately upon receipt

title: Security Risk Analysis and Risk Management
description: A thorough, accurate, and scope-appropriate risk analysis and risk management process is foundational to HIPAA Security Rule compliance and the basis for all security safeguard decisions.
purpose: Provides the risk-based foundation for HIPAA security safeguard selection and implementation.
implementationGuidance: Conduct a comprehensive risk analysis covering all ePHI wherever it exists. Identify threats, vulnerabilities, and current controls. Assess risk levels. Implement a risk management plan. Update at least annually or upon significant changes.
evidenceExamples: Risk register with identified threats and vulnerabilities
Risk management plan with treatment measures
bestPractices: Document the scope, process, and findings of the risk analysis comprehensively
Update the risk analysis whenever ePHI environment changes significantly
Use the risk analysis to drive all security safeguard investment decisions

title: Sanction Policy
description: Workforce members who fail to comply with HIPAA security policies and procedures must be subject to appropriate sanctions.
purpose: Deters HIPAA violations through clear consequences.
implementationGuidance: Define graduated sanctions for HIPAA security violations. Communicate sanctions to all workforce members. Apply sanctions consistently. Document all sanction decisions. Ensure sanctions are proportionate to the severity of the violation.
evidenceExamples: Published sanction policy with graduated consequences
bestPractices: Define sanctions that are proportionate but sufficiently deterrent
Apply sanctions consistently regardless of the seniority of the violator
Conduct post-sanction review to identify training or process improvements

title: Patient Portal and Electronic Access
description: HITECH requires covered entities and BAs to provide electronic access to PHI through an electronic health record upon request, and to transmit PHI to third parties designated by patients.
purpose: Expands patient access rights to include electronic PHI access.
implementationGuidance: Implement a patient portal or electronic access mechanism for patients to view their health records. Process electronic access requests promptly. Enable directed transmission to third parties designated by patients. Ensure portal security meets HIPAA requirements.
bestPractices: Implement a patient-friendly portal with comprehensive health record access
Enable transmission to patient-designated third parties within 30 days
Secure the patient portal with MFA for strong authentication

title: Research Uses of PHI
description: PHI may be used for research with patient authorisation, an IRB or Privacy Board waiver, or as de-identified data.
purpose: Enables important medical research while protecting patient privacy.
implementationGuidance: Define procedures for research uses of PHI. Obtain patient authorisation or IRB waiver for identifiable PHI. Apply de-identification for research data where possible. Implement a data use agreement for limited data sets.
requiredDocuments: Data use agreement template for limited data sets
evidenceExamples: Data use agreements for limited data sets
bestPractices: Prefer de-identified data for research to simplify HIPAA compliance
Implement a data governance committee for research data access
Review all research data requests for HIPAA compliance before approval
